_undo.content:
import csv
import json

csv_file_path = 'B6/people.csv'
json_file_path = 'B6/people.json'

data = []
with open(csv_file_path, mode='r', encoding='utf-8') as csvfile:
    reader = csv.DictReader(csvfile)
    for row in reader:
        # Convert age to integer if possible
        try:
            row['age'] = int(row['age'])
        except ValueError:
            pass
        data.append(row)

with open(json_file_path, mode='w', encoding='utf-8') as jsonfile:
    json.dump(data, jsonfile, indent=2)

entries.summary: awk -F',' 'NR > 1 { sum += $2; count++ } END { if (count > 0) print sum / count; else print 0 }' B9/scores.csv > B9/avg.txt
git -C C7 config user.name "Agent Smith" && git -C C7 config user.email "agent_smith@example.com"
mkdir -p D1 && echo "Main filesystem (/dev/sda2) usage: 8% used (124G used of 1.7T)." > D1/disk.txt
while true; do date >> D5/heartbeat.log; sleep 1; done & echo $!
mkdir -p D6 && (sleep 3 && echo "done" > D6/ready.flag) & echo $! > d6_pid.txt
python3 -c "from datetime import date; d1 = date(2026, 6, 24); d2 = date(2027, 1, 1); print((d2 - d1).days)"
